literal_description: A single kneeling figure in the lower left quadrant. Arms extended forward, palms ambiguous at this resolution. Upper two-thirds dominated by a gold arch with diffuse light behind or within it — light source is rendered as glow, not depicted object. Floor is dark unadorned stone. No other figures. No visible text. Palette: ochre, burnt sienna, near-black, gold, diffuse white-gold.
visual_relations: Figure positioned in lower-left, subordinate compositionally to the arch
Arch occupies upper two-thirds of frame — dominant element by area
Light radiates from within/behind arch toward figure
No framing text or identifying attributes visible

recurring_motifs.form: Kneeling figure before elevated luminous form
Gold arch as threshold or frame
contexts: Upper two-thirds, warm gold pigment, light source behind

symbolic_candidates.recurrence_evidence: Posture recurs in devotional, supplication, and submission imagery across multiple traditions
symbolic_candidates.confirming_evidence_needed: Identifying attributes, iconographic context, archive provenance

symbolic_candidates.recurrence_evidence: Gold as sacred marker in Byzantine, medieval European, and related traditions
symbolic_candidates.confirming_evidence_needed: Confirmed tradition context, period, geographic origin

witness_context.archive_limitations: This is a constructed example. No real archive provenance. Symbolic readings are illustrative of the framework, not claims about a real work.
context_available: general knowledge of Western devotional traditions

interpretive_claims.claim: The compositional arrangement spatially enacts a relation of scale and humility
interpretive_claims.basis: Small figure below, large luminous form above — spatial grammar recurring in devotional traditions

interpretive_claims.claim: Gold arch is consistent with conventions marking sacred or transcendent thresholds
interpretive_claims.basis: Color-symbolism convention in European medieval and Byzantine traditions

rejected_claims.reason: Interior state of depicted subject cannot be confirmed from image surface. Posture is consistent with prayer but also submission, grief, supplication. Prohibited inference #3.
No identifying attributes visible. Attribution without iconographic markers is fabrication.
Gold is a symbolic candidate with a range of possible meanings. Flattening to a single definition violates prohibited inference #4.

unresolved_questions: Tradition context and period not confirmed
Precise hand gesture ambiguous at available resolution

safety_notes: This is a constructed illustrative example, not an interpretation of a real artwork
All symbolic readings are conditional on archive context not provided here
Human witness with art-historical expertise recommended before treating as authoritative
